metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4698
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 8: Bcl-xL mutant that disrupts Drp1 interaction was overexpressed in hippocampal neuronal cultures, which reduced endocytosis efficacy as compared to control in synaptopHluorin assays.

"In support of a role for calcium-dependent processes, we find that inhibition of calmodulin by pharmacological or genetic means prevents Bcl-xL and Drp1 translocation to synaptic vesicles during stimulation and prevents normal vesicle pool recovery. In this form of clathrin-dependent endocytosis, we find that Bcl-xL and Drp1 act downstream of calcium influx/calmodulin."

"Interaction of Bcl-xL with Drp1 is necessary for normal vesicle retrieval; a mutation in the Bcl-xL BH2 domain decreases interaction with Drp1 and inhibits Bcl-xL-induced increase in endocytosis rate.". Experimental description: M&M: "Primary rat hippocampal neurons were prepared as described previously..."
